claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 4 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Remember, Child Benefit is assessed separately for each child.

First question: **Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who is 4 years old, and Blake, who is 14.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (age 4) and Blake (age 14). Let me gather some information about each of them.

Since both Alex and Blake are under 16, they potentially qualify as children for Child Benefit purposes. Now I need to ask a few more questions:

**First, are either Alex or Blake currently in full-time paid work (16 hours or more per week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work. They're both still children - Alex is only 4 and Blake is 14, so they're not working at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. Since neither Alex nor Blake are working, they meet that requirement.

Now, let me ask about your relationship to the children:

**Are you the parent of Alex and Blake, or do you have some other relationship to them (such as being their legal guardian, foster carer, or another type of carer)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm their parent.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to ask about living arrangements:

**Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with me, but Alex doesn't - Alex lives elsewhere.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me ask about Alex's situation since Alex doesn't live with you:

**Do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest child or £16.95 per week for additional children) towards Alex's upkeep?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help out with things like clothes and other essentials.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To be specific, the requirement is that you contribute at least the Child Benefit rate towards Alex's upkeep.

**Can you confirm that the amount you contribute towards Alex's upkeep is at least £25.60 per week (or £16.95 per week if you're claiming for another child as well)?** This could include regular payments, buying clothes, food, and other essentials that add up to this amount.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute £50 a week towards Alex's upkeep, so that's definitely more than the required amount.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, that confirms you're contributing enough towards Alex's upkeep.

Now I need to ask an important question about whether anyone else is already claiming Child Benefit for your children:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your immigration status:

**Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another type of immigration status in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Let me ask one final important question to complete the assessment:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in local authority care (meaning they've been taken into care by the council), or are they in a Young Offenders Institution?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in care or in any institution like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit based on the information you've provided.

**Good news! Based on the information you've given me, you appear to be eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's my assessment:

**For Alex (age 4):**
- ✓ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✓ Alex is not in full-time paid work
- ✓ You are Alex's parent and you're responsible for them
- ✓ Although Alex doesn't live with you, you contribute at least £50 per week towards their upkeep, which is well above the minimum Child Benefit rate required
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✓ You live in the UK and are a UK citizen
- ✓ Alex is not in local authority care or a Young Offenders Institution

**For Blake (age 14):**
- ✓ Blake is under 16 years old
- ✓ Blake is not in full-time paid work
- ✓ You are Blake's parent and Blake lives with you
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- ✓ You live in the UK and are a UK citizen
- ✓ Blake is not in local authority care or a Young Offenders Institution

**Both children meet all the eligibility criteria.** You should be able to successfully claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

**Next steps:** You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. You'll need to provide birth certificates or adoption certificates for both children when you apply.

**Please note:** If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you so much for all that information! That's really helpful to know that I can claim for both of them. I appreciate you taking the time to explain everything so clearly.
